tossups.question: This is the central person of a 1938 book that ends with a note signed “G. Loveway” and an appendix that links this non-Cuban leader to Fidel Castro in a “quest for national identity.” This devoted Catholic prevented Georges Biassou from massacring prisoners and escorted them to Le Cap. This affranchi called themself the “trunk of the tree of liberty” as he was taken by the Créole and Héros warships to the Fort de Joux. This leader defeated André Rigaud in the War of (*) Knives. Alfred Auguste Nemours helped write a book subtitled for this person by C. L. R. James. The Black Jacobins details how this man and Jean-Jacques Dessalines led a successful slave uprising in Saint-Domingue. For 10 points, name this leader of the Haitian Revolution.
tossups.question_sanitized: This is the central person of a 1938 book that ends with a note signed "G. Loveway" and an appendix that links this non-Cuban leader to Fidel Castro in a "quest for national identity." This devoted Catholic prevented Georges Biassou from massacring prisoners and escorted them to Le Cap. This affranchi called themself the "trunk of the tree of liberty" as he was taken by the Creole and Heros warships to the Fort de Joux. This leader defeated Andre Rigaud in the War of (*) Knives. Alfred Auguste Nemours helped write a book subtitled for this person by C. L. R. James. The Black Jacobins details how this man and Jean-Jacques Dessalines led a successful slave uprising in Saint-Domingue. For 10 points, name this leader of the Haitian Revolution.
tossups.answer: Toussaint L’Ouverture [or François-Dominique Toussaint Louverture; or Toussaint Bréda]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Toussaint L'Ouverture [or Francois-Dominique Toussaint Louverture; or Toussaint Breda]

bonuses.leadin: In the center of a mural, this man stands with his arms outstretched while a tornado and prairie fire approach from either side. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In the center of a mural, this man stands with his arms outstretched while a tornado and prairie fire approach from either side. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this man, who is depicted in that work with his eyes and mouth wide open and his long beard blowing in the wind. Another artist used gouache (“gwash”) and tempera for a series titled for The Legend of this man.
John Steuart Curry, artist of the mural Tragic Prelude, was one of the “Triumvirate” of this artistic movement, along with Thomas Hart Benton and Grant Wood.

In addition to his series on John Brown, Jacob Lawrence painted a series on “The Life of” this man. Lawrence’s original series of 41 paintings of this man, including a portrait in elaborate red and gold military costume, was one of his earliest major works.
parts_sanitized: Name this man, who is depicted in that work with his eyes and mouth wide open and his long beard blowing in the wind. Another artist used gouache ("gwash") and tempera for a series titled for The Legend of this man.
John Steuart Curry, artist of the mural Tragic Prelude, was one of the "Triumvirate" of this artistic movement, along with Thomas Hart Benton and Grant Wood.

In addition to his series on John Brown, Jacob Lawrence painted a series on "The Life of" this man. Lawrence's original series of 41 paintings of this man, including a portrait in elaborate red and gold military costume, was one of his earliest major works.
answers: John Brown (The Legend of John Brown is by Jacob Lawrence.)
answers_sanitized: John Brown (The Legend of John Brown is by Jacob Lawrence.)
Toussaint Louverture [or Francois-Dominique Toussaint L'Ouverture; or Toussaint Breda]

bonuses.leadin: Napoleon sent Charles Leclerc to stop this man's revolt, but yellow fever outbreaks forced him to retreat. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this general whose slave revolt evolved into the Haitian Revolution. He was ultimately succeeded in his efforts by Jean-Jacques Dessalines.
Louverture's revolution was hindered by this mixed-race man, whom he fought during the War of the Knives. Louverture sought to control this man's territory in the south of Saint-Domingue.
After Leclerc died of yellow fever, he was replaced by a cruel military commander from this family. A French count from this family led the U.S. naval blockade at the Battle of Yorktown.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Napoleon sent Charles Leclerc to stop this man's revolt, but yellow fever outbreaks forced him to retreat. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this general whose slave revolt evolved into the Haitian Revolution. He was ultimately succeeded in his efforts by Jean-Jacques Dessalines.
Louverture's revolution was hindered by this mixed-race man, whom he fought during the War of the Knives. Louverture sought to control this man's territory in the south of Saint-Domingue.
After Leclerc died of yellow fever, he was replaced by a cruel military commander from this family. A French count from this family led the U.S. naval blockade at the Battle of Yorktown.

bonuses.leadin: Along with Cecile Fatiman, this man gave a speech after drinking the blood of a black pig in the forest of Bois Caiman. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this former coachman who presided over a voodoo ceremony in 1791, which spurred an early black slave revolt around Le Cap in French Haiti.
This “Black Spartacus” led the slave revolt that succeeded where Boukman failed, becoming governor-general of an independent Haiti in 1801 before quickly giving way to his lieutenant Dessalines.

This French abbot wrote about St. Domingue in his 1770 book History of the Two Indies, which contained chapters on the East Indies, South America, West Indies, and North America and then a final chapter analyzing the future of France in the New World.
answers_sanitized: Toussaint L'Ouverture [or Francois-Dominique Toussaint Louverture; or Toussaint Breda]
Abbe Raynal [or Guillaume Thomas Francois Raynal]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Along with Cecile Fatiman, this man gave a speech after drinking the blood of a black pig in the forest of Bois Caiman. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this former coachman who presided over a voodoo ceremony in 1791, which spurred an early black slave revolt around Le Cap in French Haiti.
This "Black Spartacus" led the slave revolt that succeeded where Boukman failed, becoming governor-general of an independent Haiti in 1801 before quickly giving way to his lieutenant Dessalines.

This French abbot wrote about St. Domingue in his 1770 book History of the Two Indies, which contained chapters on the East Indies, South America, West Indies, and North America and then a final chapter analyzing the future of France in the New World.

bonuses.leadin: This man’s ancestors ruled as kings of Benin’s port city of Allada. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this leader of the Haitian Revolution who was captured by deception and deported to France before independence was achieved.
This successor of Louverture officially declared the independence of Haiti and crowned himself emperor. He ordered the massacre of all white French who remained on the island in 1804.

This leader of the free coloreds supported André Rigaud and Jean-Pierre Boyer in the War of the Knives against Louverture. After Dessalines’ murder, this man split with Henri Christophe and ruled the southern Republic of Haiti.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This man's ancestors ruled as kings of Benin's port city of Allada. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this leader of the Haitian Revolution who was captured by deception and deported to France before independence was achieved.
This successor of Louverture officially declared the independence of Haiti and crowned himself emperor. He ordered the massacre of all white French who remained on the island in 1804.

This leader of the free coloreds supported Andre Rigaud and Jean-Pierre Boyer in the War of the Knives against Louverture. After Dessalines' murder, this man split with Henri Christophe and ruled the southern Republic of Haiti.

bonuses.leadin: This man died in a prison in France after he was captured by Charles Leclerc, and he had earlier defeated Andre Rigaud in the War of the Knives. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this leader of a massive slave rebellion in the Caribbean colony of Saint-Domingue.
Toussaint Louverture led the rebellion in this modern-day country on Hispaniola, whose population in the neighboring Dominican Republic was attacked during the Parsley Massacre.

Andre Rigaud, Alexandre Petion, and Jean-Pierre Boyer were members of this class in Haiti, which was seen as the elite class and often opposed the black leaders Louverture, Dessalines, and Christophe.
answers: Republic of Haiti [or Republique d’Haiti; or Repiblik d’ Ayiti]

packet.name: Brown A - Caltech B - Colonel By

Toussaint Louverture led the rebellion in this modern-day country on Hispaniola, whose population in the neighboring Dominican Republic was attacked during the Parsley Massacre.

Andre Rigaud, Alexandre Petion, and Jean-Pierre Boyer were members of this class in Haiti, which was seen as the elite class and often opposed the black leaders Louverture, Dessalines, and Christophe.
